decisions.briefing: NASDAQ fell 0.8% while the S&P 500 barely moved — a neutral-to-risk-off session driven by growth and tech weakness. Following our established playbook, we hold cash through neutral days and wait for a genuine risk-on signal (NASDAQ up 2% or more) before deploying into our top-scoring candidates like CAR, NKTR, AUGO, and CRSP. Existing positions remain intact with no stop-loss or profit-target triggers expected today.

market_education.summary_en: The S&P 500 closed virtually flat while the NASDAQ fell 0.8%, reflecting a split session where high-multiple technology and growth names lagged while defensive sectors provided ballast. This divergence is a classic sign of intraday rotation — institutional investors trimming exposure to rate-sensitive tech stocks and rotating into steadier names in telecom, consumer staples, and value. With no major macro catalyst to break the stalemate, most market participants chose to sit on the sidelines ahead of upcoming economic data.
market_education.summary_zh: 標普500幾乎持平收市，納斯達克則下跌0.8%，呈現出典型的分化走勢——高估值科技及成長股普遍落後，而防禦性板塊則發揮穩定作用。這種背離是盤中資金輪動的典型訊號，機構投資者正在削減對利率敏感型科技股的持倉，資金轉向電信、消費必需品及價值型股票。在缺乏重大宏觀催化劑打破僵局的情況下，大多數市場參與者選擇靜待即將公布的經濟數據，觀望氣氛主導全場。

daily_lesson.explanation_en: Index divergence happens when major market indexes move in opposite directions on the same day — today the S&P 500 was flat while the NASDAQ dropped 0.8%. It signals that selling pressure is concentrated in specific sectors (here, tech and growth) while other parts of the market hold firm. Savvy investors use divergence as a clue about where institutional money is rotating, and whether overall risk appetite is rising or falling.
daily_lesson.explanation_zh: 指數背離是指主要市場指數在同一天出現不同方向走勢的現象——今日標普500幾乎持平，而納斯達克卻下跌0.8%。這表明賣壓集中於特定板塊（本次為科技及成長股），而市場其他部分則相對穩健。精明的投資者會利用背離現象來判斷機構資金的輪動方向，以及整體市場的風險偏好是上升還是下降。
